items.title: Mexico’s Victory Over Ecuador Made the Ground Shake. Was It an Artificial Earthquake?
items.summary: Fans’ euphoric reactions to the Mexican national team’s recent victory in the 2026 World Cup caused a series of unusual vibrations that were detected by seismic warning systems.

items.title: Goose, a New Gay Dating App, Appears to Be a Psyop
items.summary: Touted as a less-hookup-focused Grindr, Goose is an invite-only space for gay men. The problem is the people promoting it don’t seem real.

items.title: An Explosion Knocked Out Anduril’s Rocket Motor Test Site in Mississippi
items.summary: The incident could disrupt a key part of the defense company’s rocket motor business, which designs and tests prototype motors for military customers.

items.title: Bending Spoons defies SaaS slump, surges 40% on first day of trading
items.summary: The company has grown rapidly by acquiring and revamping last-generation tech brands like AOL, Eventbrite, Evernote, Meetup, and Vimeo.

items.title: After $18B IPO, Bending Spoons founder says success comes from minimizing luck
items.summary: The co-founders of Bending Spoons, the Italian company quietly buying beloved, ailing Internet brands, learned big lessons from their own startup's failure.

items.title: WhatsApp usernames are already raising impersonation red flags
items.summary: Meta says usernames improve privacy, but critics question whether its safeguards can prevent impersonation.

items.title: Aunt of Venezuelan boy pulled from rubble tells BBC she will give him 'mother's warmth'
items.summary: Kleiber Moran, 2, was rescued on Wednesday, six days after last week's deadly earthquakes.

items.title: Ukrainian charged in Germany over Nord Stream blasts
items.summary: Ukraine denies involvement in the case which may have serious implications for its relationship with Germany.

items.title: US blocks long-term renewal of North American trade deal
items.summary: US blocks 16-year North America trade deal renewal, triggering annual rolling reviews

items.title: Prolific tech analyst Dan Ives is exiting Wedbush for a new venture
items.summary: Ives said the new firm will seek to build what he described as a "modern merchant bank."

items.title: Warsh faces multiple alternative inflation signs as Fed charts new course
items.summary: Warsh has said that inflation is a "choice." The same could also be true of how inflation is measured.

items.title: Inflation peaked in May as energy prices fell in June, Kalshi traders think
items.summary: Speculators on the prediction market platform give less than 30% odds to inflation peaking above 4.2% in 2026.

items.title: Stocks Sink in Broad AI Rout Sparked by China's DeepSeek
items.summary: U.S. stocks were mostly lower, with the Nasdaq leading declines as makers of AI infrastructure suffered steep falls, many in the double digits. Nvidia was down 16%.

items.title: Comex Gold, Silver Settle Lower
items.summary: Gold settled 1.4% lower, down two of the past three sessions, and silver fell 2.5%, down three of the past four sessions.

items.title: DeepSeek Won't Sink U.S. AI Titans
items.summary: Panic fueling the selloff of Nvidia, Broadcom and other tech giants is overblown.

items.title: The Memo: Left’s winning streak extends in Colorado despite Trump, centrist attacks
items.summary: The leftist tide coursing through Democratic politics reached Colorado on Tuesday, washing away longtime Rep. Diana DeGette (D-Colo.) and putting paid to the gubernatorial hopes of Sen. Michael Bennet (D-Colo.). In the aftermath, Democrats are asking urgent questions about the direction their party ought to take.  Voices on the left say insurgent candidates are meeting…

items.title: Trump talks to AI Teddy Roosevelt during presidential library visit
items.summary: President Trump had a little chat with an artificial intelligence version of one of his predecessors Wednesday while touring the new Theodore Roosevelt Presidential Library in North Dakota. “Every day a president faces storms most people never see, but if you keep your nerve and remember the nation comes first, you get through,” the AI…

items.title: Trump vows to give long July 4 address even if it is 107 degrees
items.summary: President Trump on Wednesday vowed that he will deliver a long speech on the National Mall for the U.S.’s 250th birthday, even as forecasters anticipate temperatures climbing to 107 degrees. “By the way, on July 4th, it’s going to be approximately 107 degrees out, and I’m going to go, and I’m going to make a…

items.title: Former retail giant closed over 1,000 locations
items.summary: Former retail giant closed over 1,000 locations

items.title: IPO Edge NYSE Firesides Scheduled for July 28
items.summary: IPO Edge NYSE Firesides Scheduled for July 28

items.title: Webull Launches Crypto Trading In Canada
items.summary: Webull Launches Crypto Trading In Canada

items.title: Scientists discover a completely different way to fight viruses
items.summary: Researchers have uncovered an unexpected antiviral defense system in sea anemones that works very differently from the one humans use. The discovery suggests evolution developed multiple ways to combat viruses, challenging long-held ideas about how animal immune systems evolved.

items.title: Scientists may have finally found how Alzheimer's spreads through the brain
items.summary: A common brain protein may be giving Alzheimer’s disease an unexpected way to spread, carrying toxic Tau proteins from damaged neurons into healthy ones. By blocking these harmful protein packages before they reach new cells, researchers believe it may one day be possible to slow the disease's relentless progression.

items.title: Scientists say creatine may help fight depression
items.summary: Creatine is best known as a muscle-building supplement, but scientists are now investigating whether it could also help treat depression by boosting the brain's energy supply. A new review examined five randomized clinical trials involving 238 participants and found mixed results. Two studies, both involving women with major depressive disorder, reported that adding creatine to standard treatment improved symptoms, while three others found no meaningful benefit.

items.title: Young gulls’ drab plumage may help them avoid adult attacks
items.summary: Fake, painted decoys suggest immature coloring acts as a social signal, reducing aggression from territorial nesting gulls.

items.title: This space telescope is falling. A robotic spacecraft may save it
items.summary: A private rocket mission aims to boost NASA’s Swift telescope before its orbit decays, extending its hunt for gamma-ray bursts.

items.title: A new species of walking shark has been found in Papua New Guinea
items.summary: Walking sharks crawl on their fins across reefs and even out into tide pools. The newfound Dudgeon walking shark brings the known species count to 10.
